EconomicCalendarEvent.title: EconomicCalendarEvent
EconomicCalendarEvent.description: Represents a single economic event fetched from external calendar sources, with times converted to Bucharest time.

id.description: Unique identifier for the EconomicCalendarEvent entity.

title.description: The concise name or title of the economic event.

description.description: A more detailed description of the economic event.

dateTime.description: The date and time of the event, adjusted to Bucharest, Romania timezone.

currency.description: The currency primarily affected by this economic event (e.g., USD, EUR, JPY).

impact.description: The expected impact level of the event on the market (e.g., 'High', 'Medium', 'Low').

actual.description: The actual reported value for the economic indicator.

forecast.description: The forecasted value for the economic indicator.

previous.description: The previous period's value for the economic indicator.

source.description: The source from which this event data was aggregated (e.g., 'Investing.com', 'ForexFactory').

link.description: An optional URL linking to the original event details on the source website.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the event record was first created/ingested.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the event record was last updated.

WeeklyMarketOverview.title: WeeklyMarketOverview
WeeklyMarketOverview.description: Stores the AI-generated weekly market overview, including analysis and probability.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WeeklyMarketOverview entity.

weekStartDate.description: The starting date of the week this overview covers.

weekEndDate.description: The ending date of the week this overview covers.

overviewText.description: The AI-generated textual summary and market outlook for the week.

successProbability.description: A percentage representing the AI agent's estimated success probability for its overview.

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when this weekly market overview was generated by the AI.

DailyMarketAnalysis.title: DailyMarketAnalysis
DailyMarketAnalysis.description: Stores the AI-generated concise daily market analysis, including potential bias.

id.description: Unique identifier for the DailyMarketAnalysis entity.

analysisDate.description: The specific date for which this daily analysis is provided.

analysisText.description: The AI-generated short analysis for the specific day.

marketBias.description: The potential market bias suggested by the AI agent for that day (e.g., 'Bullish', 'Bearish', 'Neutral').

weeklyOverviewId.description: Reference to the WeeklyMarketOverview this daily analysis belongs to. (Relationship: WeeklyMarketOverview 1:N DailyMarketAnalysis)

generatedAt.description: Timestamp when this daily market analysis was generated by the AI.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated weekly market overviews. All documents in this collection are publicly readable, providing a high-level summary of market outlooks. No user-specific authorization is required.

params.name: weeklyOverviewId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific weekly market overview.

definition.description: Stores AI-generated daily market analyses, nested under their respective weekly overview. All documents in this subcollection are publicly readable. The document includes 'weeklyOverviewId' to explicitly link it to its parent, enhancing data retrieval flexibility.

params.name: weeklyOverviewId
params.description: The unique identifier of the parent weekly market overview.

params.name: dailyAnalysisId
params.description: Unique identifier for a specific daily market analysis.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed to be highly secure, scalable, and debuggable, adhering strictly to the core design principles and strategy mandates. Given the application's requirement for publicly accessible AI-generated market analyses and an explicit preference for MongoDB for the high-volume 'EconomicCalendarEvent' data, only the AI-generated content is modeled in Firestore.

**Authorization Independence:** This structure achieves Authorization Independence because all documents in the `/weeklyMarketOverviews` collection and its `/dailyMarketAnalyses` subcollection are intended for public read access. There are no user-specific permissions or complex roles applied to this data. Consequently, security rules will not require any `get()` calls to check parent document attributes or user roles, simplifying them to `allow read: if true;`. This approach ensures atomic operations and simplifies debugging by removing inter-document dependencies for authorization.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The design directly supports QAPs by enforcing **Structural Segregation**. Each collection (and subcollection) has a homogeneous security posture: all documents within `/weeklyMarketOverviews` are publicly readable, and similarly, all documents within `/weeklyMarketOverviews/{weeklyOverviewId}/dailyMarketAnalyses` are publicly readable. Because every document in these collections shares the same access requirements, a `list` operation will retrieve all available documents without the security rules having to filter results. This makes `list` queries efficient and predictable, as the rules engine does not act as a data filter, but rather as an access gate for the entire collection/subcollection.
